_comment: Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/Stress Incontinence Surgery/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). SU062A is the HBP 2.0 code for this procedure.

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of management
Cystoscopy/ USG /Urodynamic study to confirm the diagnosis

ppd_preauth.q: Was there any evidence of stress urinary inconsistence in the cystoscopy report?

common_queries: No evidence of stress urinary incontinence found in the cystoscopy report
Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of management not submitted or incomplete
Cystoscopy/ USG /Urodynamic study not submitted or not confirming the diagnosis
Barcode/invoice for Implant used not submitted at claim stage

clinical_key_pointers: Stress urinary Incontinence: (SUI) Is an Unintentional loss if urine during physical activity or coughing, laughing, sneezing, running or heavy lifting.
Urinary incontinence can originate from the urethra or bladder or from a combination of both.
Bladder causes are detrusor hyperactivity or hypoactivity.
Urethral causes are intrinsic urethral sphincter deficiency with or without urethral hypermobility.
surgical_options: Open (Anterior repair, Transabdominal & Transvaginal suspension, Sling procedures)
